'''Éloi Pélissier''' (born 18 June 1991) is a French professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[Baroudeurs de Pia XIII|Pia]] in the [[Super XIII]] and [[France national rugby league team|France]] at international level.

He has previously played for the [[Catalans Dragons]] and the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Super League]], the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[Super League]], [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and [[FC Lézignan XIII|Lézignan ]] in the [[Super XIII|Elite One Championship]].

==Background==
Pelissier was born in [[Perpignan]], [[Languedoc-Roussillon]], [[France]]. He is of [[Spaniards|Span]]ish descent.

==Playing career==
Pélissier had a trial with [[Wigan Warriors]] in 2008 in their Under 18s squad. He made 2 appearances against [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] playing centre but failed to earn a contract.<ref>{{cite news 
|title = Wigan RL History - 2008 Academy Team (Under 18s)
|work = www.cherryandwhite.co.uk
|place =UK
|publisher = www.cherryandwhite.co.uk
|date = 2008-09-14
|url = http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=735
|accessdate = 2010-10-07}}</ref>

In 2009 and 2010 Pélissier played for [[AS Saint Estève|St Estève]] in the [[Elite One Championship]]. He has repressed [[France national rugby league team|France]] at Under 20s level and was included in the French train-on squad for [[2010 European Cup|2010 Alitalia European Cup]].<ref>{{cite news|place=UK|url=http://www.rlef.eu.com/news.php?id=1439|title=Nine Newcomers In French Squad|publisher=Rugby League European Federation|work=rlef.eu.com|date=2010-09-02|accessdate=2010-10-07}}</ref>

He joined the [[Catalans Dragons]] in 2011. Pelissier made his [[Super League]] début for the Dragons in a 22-16 defeat by St Helens in 2011, coming off the bench to play at hooker.<ref name="Catalans 16-22 St Helens">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_facts/0,19935,11070_3343364,00.html|title=Catalans 16-22 St Helens|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2011-03-05|accessdate=2011-03-19}}</ref> He then played the week after, in a 31-18 victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], and went on to establish himself as a regular in the Dragons side.<ref name="Hull KR 18-31 Catalans">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_facts/0,19935,11070_3343359,00.html|title=Hull KR 18-31 Catalans|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2011-02-27|accessdate=2011-03-19}}</ref>

He signed with [[Lézignan Sangliers]] for the 2017-18 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://translate.google.com.au/translate?hl=en&sl=fr&u=http://www.fcl13.fr/actualites-fcl-xiii/1946-eloi-pelissier-ben-pomeroy-et-dgino-waeldo-dernieres-recrues-du-fcl&prev=search |title=Elo Pelissier, Ben Pomero and Dgino Waledo final FCL recruits |website=FCL13 |date=1 January 2017 |accessdate=1 January 2017}}</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique===
On 2 November 2020, it was reported that he had signed for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship]].  In round 23 of the 2022 Super League season, Pélissier scored two tries for Toulouse Olympique in a 32-18 loss against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12209/12670907/super-league-warrington-wolves-move-step-closer-to-survival-with-32-18-victory-over-toulouse/|title=Super League: Warrington Wolves move step closer to survival with 32-18 victory over Toulouse|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=www.rugby-league.com|date=2 Nov 2020|accessdate=12 Jan 2021|title=TOULOUSE OLYMPIQUE SIGN ELOI PÉLISSIER|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/57294/toulouse-olympique-sign-eloi-plissier}}</ref>
He played a total of 19 games for Toulouse in the 2022 Super League season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated back to the RFL Championship.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/toulouse-suffering-untold-damage-due-to-relegation-from-super-league/|title=Toulouse suffering "untold damage" due to relegation from Super League|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
On 15 October 2023, he played in Toulouse Olympique's upset loss in the [[Million Pound Game]] against the [[London Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67105947|title=Championship Grand Final: Toulouse 14-18 London Broncos - Capital club back in Super League|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 8 May 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]], joining from [[Super XIII]] club [[Baroudeurs de Pia XIII]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=8 May 2025|author=George Riley|title=Championship heavyweights snap up former Super League star|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/championship-heavyweights-snap-up-former-super-league-star}}</ref>

==International career==
Pélissier made his full international debut for [[France national rugby league team|France]] in 2011. He played for France in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] and [[2014 European Cup]]. Pélissier returned to the international stage in the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations#France vs England|end of year test match]] against England in Avignon. He scored France's lone try in the match as the French went on to be lose 6-40. During the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|2017 World Cup]] in [[Perth, Australia]], Pélissier was expelled from the French delegation after breaking curfew.<ref>{{cite web | url =https://www.lindependant.fr/2017/11/07/eloi-pelissier-sur-son-exclusion-du-xiii-de-france-je-me-suis-enfonce-au-lieu-de-dire-la-verite,3067625.php | title =Eloi Pélissier sur son exclusion du XIII de France: "Je me suis enfoncé au lieu de dire la vérité" | author=B.O. | date =7 November 2017  | website =lindependant.fr | language =fr | access-date =April 2, 2023}}</ref>
